entities.name: GENOMICS
entities.description: The exploration of an organism's entire DNA composition to understand genome structure, function, and evolution

entities.name: ORGANISM
entities.description: The biological entity whose DNA composition is explored in genomics

entities.name: HIGH-THROUGHPUT SEQUENCING TECHNOLOGIES
entities.description: Technologies that facilitate the generation of extensive genomic data

entities.name: GENOMIC DATA
entities.description: Extensive data including DNA sequences, gene expression profiles, and epigenetic modifications

entities.name: DNA SEQUENCES
entities.description: A component of genomic data consisting of the nucleotide order of DNA

entities.name: GENE EXPRESSION PROFILES
entities.description: A component of genomic data reflecting the activity levels of genes

entities.name: EPIGENETIC MODIFICATIONS
entities.description: A component of genomic data involving regulatory mechanisms like DNA methylation and histone modifications

entities.name: DNA SEQUENCING TECHNOLOGIES
entities.description: Technologies used for the rapid and cost-effective sequencing of DNA

entities.name: NEXT-GENERATION SEQUENCING
entities.description: A type of DNA sequencing technology that revolutionized rapid sequencing

entities.name: WHOLE-GENOME SEQUENCING
entities.description: A technique for sequencing the entire genome of an organism

entities.name: RNA SEQUENCING
entities.description: A technique (RNA-Seq) used to quantify gene expression and detect transcripts

entities.name: CHROMATIN IMMUNOPRECIPITATION SEQUENCING
entities.description: A sequencing technique used to analyze protein-DNA interactions

entities.name: SINGLE-CELL SEQUENCING
entities.description: A technique that enables the exploration of individual cells to unveil cellular heterogeneity

entities.name: CELLULAR HETEROGENEITY
entities.description: The variation in gene expression or composition between individual cells within tissues

entities.name: FUNCTIONAL GENOMICS TECHNIQUES
entities.description: Techniques used for targeted gene manipulation to unveil gene roles

entities.name: CRISPR-CAS9
entities.description: A specific functional genomics tool used for targeted gene manipulation

entities.name: GENES
entities.description: The basic units of heredity whose roles are studied via functional genomics

entities.name: THIRD-GENERATION SEQUENCING
entities.description: Sequencing technology that generates massive datasets with high precision

entities.name: SEQUENCE ALIGNMENT
entities.description: An analytical tool used to analyze genomic data by aligning sequences

entities.name: ASSEMBLY ALGORITHMS
entities.description: Tools used to reconstruct entire genomes from genomic data

entities.name: VARIANT CALLING
entities.description: A process used to identify genetic variations such as SNPs and indels

entities.name: SINGLE-NUCLEOTIDE POLYMORPHISMS
entities.description: A type of genetic variation identified through variant calling

entities.name: INSERTIONS/DELETIONS
entities.description: Genetic variations known as indels identified through variant calling

entities.name: TRANSCRIPTOMICS
entities.description: The study of the transcriptome, providing a dynamic portrayal of cellular processes

entities.name: GENE EXPRESSION LEVELS
entities.description: The quantified levels of gene activity measured through transcriptomics

entities.name: ALTERNATIVE SPLICING EVENTS
entities.description: Events where different exons are joined, uncovered by transcriptomics

entities.name: NOVEL TRANSCRIPTS
entities.description: Previously unknown RNA transcripts detected by transcriptomics

entities.name: DNA METHYLATION
entities.description: A specific type of epigenetic modification influencing gene expression

entities.name: HISTONE MODIFICATIONS
entities.description: A specific type of epigenetic modification influencing gene expression
